sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After RIGHT, the P-shape moved back to cols 15-29 (from cols 9-23). So RIGHT moved the P-shape RIGHT by 6 columns. The character stayed at cols 45-59.
So the control scheme is:
- LEFT moves P-shape LEFT by 6 columns
- RIGHT moves P-shape RIGHT by 6 columns
- DOWN doesn't work (blocked)
- UP probably doesn't work either
The P-shape is at cols 15-29, and the Y-shape is at cols 3-17. To overlap, I need to move the P-shape LEFT by some amount. But the P-shape moves by 6 columns per LEFT action.
Current P-shape: cols 15-29
Y-shape: cols 3-17
If I move LEFT once: P-shape cols 9-23
If I move LEFT twice: P-shape cols 3-17
So 2 LEFT moves should position the P-shape at cols 3-17, which matches the Y-shape's horizontal position. But the P-shape is at rows 18-29, and the Y-shape is at rows 42-53. So the vertical positions don't match.
Wait, but DOWN doesn't work. So how do I move the P-shape vertically? Maybe I need to use a different action like SPACE or MOUSE.
Actually, let me reconsider. In Level 1, the character could move UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT. In Level 2, the P-shape can only move LEFT/RIGHT. Maybe the goal is different in Level 2.
Let me check if the P-shape and Y-shape have the same shape. From the boundaries:
- P-shape: [[18, 21], [18, 29], [20, 29], [20, 24], [21, 23], [29, 23], [29, 15], [27, 15], [27, 20], [26, 21]]
- Y-shape: [[42, 9], [42, 17], [44, 17], [44, 12], [45, 11], [53, 11], [53, 3], [51, 3], [51, 8], [50, 9]]
The P-shape spans rows 18-29 and cols 15-29.
The Y-shape spans rows 42-53 and cols 3-17.
The row spans are different (18-29 vs 42-53), and the col spans are different (15-29 vs 3-17). But the shapes might be similar.
Let me compare the relative shapes:
P-shape boundary (relative to row 18, col 15):
[[0, 6], [0, 14], [2, 14], [2, 9], [3, 8], [11, 8], [11, 0], [9, 0], [9, 5], [8, 6]]
Y-shape boundary (relative to row 42, col 3):
[[0, 6], [0, 14], [2, 14], [2, 9], [3, 8], [11, 8], [11, 0], [9, 0], [9, 5], [8, 6]]
They match! So the P-shape and Y-shape have the same shape. The goal is to position the P-shape so that it overlaps with the Y-shape.
The P-shape is at rows 18-29, cols 15-29.
The Y-shape is at rows 42-53, cols 3-17.
To overlap, the P-shape needs to be at rows 42-53, cols 3-17. But the P-shape can only move LEFT/RIGHT, not UP/DOWN. So how do I move it vertically?
Wait, maybe I need to move the character (B) instead of the P-shape. Let me check if the character can move.
Actually, in the previous actions, LEFT and RIGHT moved the P-shape, not the character. DOWN didn't work. Maybe the character can move, but I need to target it differently.
Let me try clicking on the character using MOUSE to see if it becomes the active target.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the Level 1 behavior. In Level 1, I moved the character using LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN, and the P-shape mirrored. In Level 2, LEFT/RIGHT moves the P-shape directly. Maybe the control scheme is different in Level 2.
Let me try SPACE to see if it switches the active target.
